checks.a_analysis: The output text follows the source document exactly from the header down to the signature lines. No invented sections, headings, or body paragraphs are present. The structure used for signatures (a table with 'Field' and 'Value' headers) is an acceptable accessibility enhancement permitted under the rules.
The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this absence and contains no hallucinated contact details.
All sections from the source document (headers, county seal image, title, all five WHEREAS clauses, the resolving clause, adoption date, and signee names/titles) are present in the output JSON.

The signature lines from the source are represented in a table in the output. The table contains two data rows corresponding to the two signees (Taylor McCurry and Steve Pitman) identified in the source. No rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each paragraph and clause has been captured in its entirety. The conversion preserves the complex phrasing of the legislative clauses without truncation.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content is highly faithful to the source. Names, dates (February 21st, 2023), and specific act titles match the source document exactly. Punctuation at the end of clauses (e.g., 'and;') is also accurately captured.

checks.a_analysis: The document content is captured in a linear sequence. Each text block from the source appears exactly once in the semantic text elements of the JSON. The signature block names are present in the table (element index 12). While the handwritten signatures are also captured as cropped images at the end of the document, these represent the graphical original and do not constitute duplicative text content in the semantic hierarchy.

The document uses a consistent heading hierarchy. It starts with an H2 for the main title ('RESOLUTION SUPPORTING...') and follows with an H3 for the resolving clause ('NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED...'). There are no level skips (e.g., H2 to H4) in the output. Starting the document with H2 is an acceptable normalization adjustment.

There are no formal data tables in the source document. The signature block, which uses a side-by-side layout in the PDF, has been converted into a structured table (element index 12). This correctly preserves the grouping of names and titles, and does not represent a corruption of a source table.

The reading order follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the document: location headers, title, preamble clauses, the resolution itself, and finally the adoption statement and signatures. While the official seal image (index 13) has been moved to the end of the document, this is a minor reordering often used for accessibility and does not impact the logical flow of the resolution text.
The document contains no lists; the preamble 'WHEREAS' statements are correctly identified as individual paragraphs.
No links are present in the source, and none have been incorrectly added to the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source document is a single, cohesive page resolution. The HTML output significantly disrupts this visual organization by fragmenting the content into multiple redundant sections: a plain text block, a 'Signatures' table, a separate county seal image, an image snippet of signatures, and finally a large high-contrast/inverted reconstruction of the entire document. This layout is confusing and does not maintain the visual flow or intent of the original formal document.
checks.b_evidence_source: A single-page formal resolution with a unified layout containing header text, a seal, body text, and a signature block.
checks.b_evidence_output: The content is spread across several disparate sections: a text block (lines 4-29), a Signatures table, individual image assets for the seal and signatures, and a final large section repeating the whole document in white text on a black background.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML includes a 'Signatures' table. This table is rendered correctly with clearly defined borders, headers, and legible text. Although the 'Value' column is empty, the table structure itself is not broken.

Meaningful images from the source, specifically the Mitchell County seal and the handwritten signatures, are present in the HTML output. There are no signs of broken image icons or missing meaningful graphical content.

The entire body content of the resolution is duplicated. The text from 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION...' down to the adoption date appears as a plain text block at the top of the HTML page and is then repeated in its entirety within the high-contrast section at the bottom of the page.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text of the resolution: 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF MITCHELL COUNTY 911 DISPATCHERS... WHEREAS... NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED... Adopted this the 21st day of February, 2023.'
checks.b_evidence_output: The text block starting near the top of the screenshot (under the 'MITCHELL COUNTY' header) and the identical content block appearing again within the large black-and-white inverted section at the bottom.
checks.b_evidence_location: The first text section (lines 4-29) and the final large document block at the bottom of the screenshot.
